pou.name: MC_GetTorqueLimit
pou.documentation: This fonction blocks returns the axis torque limits

pou.name: MC_SetTorqueLimit
pou.documentation: This fonction blocks defines the axis torque limits

pou.name: MC_Power
pou.documentation: This Function Block controls the power stage (On or Off).

pou.name: MC_Home
pou.documentation: This Function Block commands the axis to perform the «search home» sequence. The details of this sequence are manufacturer dependent and can be set by the axis’ parameters. The ‘Position’ input is used to set the absolute position when reference signal is detected. This Function Block completes at ‘Standstill’ if it was started in ‘Standstill’.

pou.name: MC_Stop
pou.documentation: This Function Block commands a controlled motion stop and transfers the axis to the state ‘Stopping’. It aborts any ongoing Function Block execution. While the axis is in state ‘Stopping’, no other FB can perform any motion on the same axis. After the axis has reached ‘Velocity’ zero, the ‘Done’ output is set to TRUE immediately. The axis remains in the state ‘Stopping’ as long as ‘Execute’ is still TRUE or ‘Velocity’ zero is not yet reached. As soon as ‘Done’ is SET and ‘Execute’ is FALSE the axis goes to state ‘Standstill’.

pou.name: MC_Halt
pou.documentation: This Function Block commands a controlled motion stop. The axis is moved to the state ‘DiscreteMotion’, until the velocity is zero. With the ‘Done’ output set, the state is transferred to ‘Standstill’.

pou.name: MC_MoveAbsolute
pou.documentation: This Function Block commands a controlled motion to a specified absolute position.

pou.name: MC_MoveRelative
pou.documentation: This Function Block commands a controlled motion of a specified distance relative to the set position at the time of the execution.

pou.name: MC_MoveAdditive
pou.documentation: This Function Block commands a controlled motion of a specified relative distance additional to the most recent commanded position in the axis state ‘DiscreteMotion’. The most recent commanded position may be the result of a previous MC_MoveAdditive motion which was aborted. If the FB is activated in the axis state ‘ContinuousMotion’, the specified relative distance is added to the set position at the time of the execution.

pou.name: MC_MoveSuperimposed
pou.documentation: This Function Block commands a controlled motion of a specified relative distance additional to an existing motion. The existing Motion is not interrupted, but is superimposed by the additional motion.

pou.name: MC_HaltSuperimposed
pou.documentation: This Function Block commands a halt to all superimposed motions of the axis. The underlying motion is not interrupted.

pou.name: MC_MoveVelocity
pou.documentation: This Function Block commands a never ending controlled motion at a specified velocity.

pou.name: MC_MoveContinuousAbsolute
pou.documentation: This Function Block commands a controlled motion to a specified absolute position ending with the specified velocity.

pou.name: MC_MoveContinuousRelative
pou.documentation: This Function Block commands a controlled motion of a specified relative distance ending with the specified velocity.

pou.name: MC_TorqueControl
pou.documentation: This Function Block continuously exerts a torque or force of the specified magnitude. This magnitude is approached using a defined ramp (‘TorqueRamp’), and the Function Block sets the ‘InTorque’ output if the commanded torque level is reached. This function block is applicable for force and torque. When there is no external load, force is applicable. Positive torque is in the positive direction of velocity.

pou.name: MC_PositionProfile
pou.documentation: This Function Block commands a time-position locked motion profile

pou.name: MC_VelocityProfile
pou.documentation: This Function Block commands a time-velocity locked motion profile. The velocity in the final element in the profile should be maintained. The state remains ‘ContinuousMotion’.

pou.name: MC_AccelerationProfile
pou.documentation: This Function Block commands a time-acceleration locked motion profile. After finalizing the acceleration profile, the acceleration goes to 0 (and typically the final velocity is maintained). It stays in the state ‘ContinuousMotion’.

pou.name: MC_SetPosition
pou.documentation: This Function Block shifts the coordinate system of an axis by manipulating both the set-point position as well as the actual position of an axis with the same value without any movement caused. (Re-calibration with same following error). This can be used for instance for a reference situation. This Function Block can also be used during motion without changing the commanded position, which is now positioned in the shifted coordinate system.

pou.name: MC_SetOverride
pou.documentation: This Function Block sets the values of override for the whole axis, and all functions that are working on that axis. The override parameters contribute as a factor to the calculation of the commanded velocity, acceleration and jerk of the motion.

pou.name: MC_ReadParameter
pou.documentation: This Function Block returns the value of a vendor specific parameter. The returned Value has to be converted to Real if necessary. If not possible, the vendor has to supply a vendor specific FB to read the parameter.

pou.name: MC_ReadBoolParameter
pou.documentation: This Function Block returns the value of a vendor specific parameter with datatype BOOL.

pou.name: MC_WriteParameter
pou.documentation: This Function Block modifies the value of a vendor specific parameter.

pou.name: MC_WriteBoolParameter
pou.documentation: This Function Block modifies the value of a vendor specific parameter of type BOOL.

pou.name: MC_ReadDigitalInput
pou.documentation: This Function Block gives access to the value of the input, referenced by the datatype AXIS_REF. It provides the value of the referenced input (BOOL)

pou.name: MC_ReadDigitalOutput
pou.documentation: This Function Block provides access to the value of a digital output, referenced by the datatype AXIS_REF. It provides the value of the referenced output (BOOL).

pou.name: MC_WriteDigitalOutput
pou.documentation: This Function Block writes a value to the output referenced by the argument ‘Output’ once (with rising edge of Execute).

pou.name: MC_ReadActualPosition
pou.documentation: This Function Block returns the actual position.

pou.name: MC_ReadActualVelocity
pou.documentation: This Function Block returns the value of the actual velocity as long as ‘Enable’ is set. ‘Valid’ is true when the dataoutput ‘Velocity’ is valid. If ‘Enable’ is Reset, the data loses its validity, and all outputs are reset, no matter if new data is available.

pou.name: MC_ReadActualTorque
pou.documentation: This Function Block returns the value of the actual torque or force as long as ‘Enable’ is set. ‘Valid’ is true when the data-output ‘Torque’ is valid. If ‘Enable’ is Reset, the data loses its validity, and ‘Valid’ is also reset, no matter if new data is available.

pou.name: MC_ReadStatus
pou.documentation: This Function Block returns in detail the status of the state diagram of the selected axis.

pou.name: MC_ReadMotionState
pou.documentation: This Function Block returns in detail the status of the axis with respect to the motion currently in progress.

pou.name: MC_ReadAxisInfo
pou.documentation: This Function Block reads information concerning an axis, like modes, inputs directly related to the axis, and certain status information.

pou.name: MC_ReadAxisError
pou.documentation: This Function Block presents general axis errors not relating to the Function Blocks. (for instance axis errors, drive errors, communication errors)

pou.name: MC_Reset
pou.documentation: This Function Block makes the transition from the state ‘ErrorStop’ to ‘Standstill’ or ‘Disabled’ by resetting all internal axis-related errors – it does not affect the output of the FB instances.

pou.name: MC_DigitalCamSwitch
pou.documentation: This Function Block is the analogy to switches on a motor shaft: it commands a group of discrete output bits to switch in analogy to a set of mechanical cam controlled switches connected to an axis. Forward and backward movements are allowed.

pou.name: MC_TouchProbe
pou.documentation: This Function Block is used to record an axis position at a trigger event

pou.name: MC_AbortTrigger
pou.documentation: This Function Block is used to abort function blocks which are connected to trigger events (e.g. MC_TouchProbe)

pou.name: MC_CamTableSelect
pou.documentation: This Function Block selects the CAM tables by setting the connections to the relevant tables

pou.name: MC_CamIn
pou.documentation: This Function Block engages the CAM

pou.name: MC_CamOut
pou.documentation: This Function Block disengages the Slave axis from the Master axis immediately

pou.name: MC_GearIn
pou.documentation: This Function Block commands a ratio between the VELOCITY of the slave and master axis.

pou.name: MC_GearOut
pou.documentation: This Function Block disengages the Slave axis from the Master axis

pou.name: MC_GearInPos
pou.documentation: This Function Block commands a gear ratio between the position of the slave and master axes from the synchronization point onwards.

pou.name: MC_PhasingAbsolute
pou.documentation: This Function Block creates an absolute phase shift in the master position of a slave axis. The master position is shifted in relation to the real physical position. This is analogous to opening a coupling on the master shaft for a moment, and is used to delay or advance an axis to its master. The phase shift is seen from the slave. The master does not know that there is a phase shift experienced by the slave (MasterPos as seen from the SlaveAxis = PhysicalMasterPos + PhaseShiftValueSlaveAxis, the phase shift value has the character of a position offset) The phase shift remains until another ‘Phasing’ command changes it again.

pou.name: MC_PhasingRelative
pou.documentation: This Function Block creates a phase shift in the master position of a slave axis relative to the existing phase shift. The master position is shifted in relation to the real physical position. This is analogous to opening a coupling on the master shaft for a moment, and is used to delay or advance an axis to its master. The phase shift is seen from the slave. The master does not know that there is a phase shift experienced by the slave. (MasterPos as seen from SlaveAxis = PhysicalMasterPos + PhaseShiftValueSlaveAxis, the phase shift value has the character of a position offset) The phase shift remains until another ‘Phasing’ command changes it again. Relative phase shifts are added to each other for the applicable phase shift

pou.name: MC_CombineAxes
pou.documentation: This Function Block combines the motion of 2 axes into a third axis with selectable combination method. Basically it is a calculation of a new position setpoint based on the 2 position setpoints of the input axes. This FB is reflected in the state diagram like a synchronized motion type. As application example one can work with a separate profile synchronized to an object on a moving belt, or a rotating knife with flexible covered distance to be cut.

pou.name: MC_Sim
pou.documentation: This allocates a simulation Axis, for testing. One axis per instanciated FB.

pou.name: MC_CreateTrigger
pou.documentation: Create MC_TRIGGER_REF type, with given parameters. PositiveEdge and NegativeEdged default to TRUE.

pou.name: MC_CreateOutput
pou.documentation: This allocates an output object. One output per instanciated FB.

pou.name: MC_ToIntArray
pou.documentation: This generate an array of 6 INT from inputs.

pou.name: MC_ReadOutputBits
pou.documentation: This returns bits contained in MC_OUTPUT_REF.

pou.name: MC_ReadOutputBit
pou.documentation: This returns bits contained in MC_OUTPUT_REF.

pou.name: MC_TP_REF_FROM_CSV
pou.documentation: Load a CSV file, and present it as variable of type MC_TP_REF.

pou.name: MC_TV_REF_FROM_CSV
pou.documentation: Load a CSV file, and present it as variable of type MC_TV_REF.

pou.name: MC_TA_REF_FROM_CSV
pou.documentation: Load a CSV file, and present it as variable of type MC_TA_REF.

pou.name: MC_CAMSWITCH_REF_FROM_CSV
pou.documentation: Load a CSV file, and present it as variable of type MC_CAMSWITCH_REF.

pou.name: MC_CAM_REF_FROM_CSV
pou.documentation: Load a CSV file, and present it as variable of type MC_CAM_REF.
